calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from The Presiding Judge Department 206 for Entry of Judgment.
Department 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

David P. Wasserman, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Theodore Vinther and Mary Vinther.
No appearance on behalf or by The Estate of Norman S. Wong, through its Executor, Diane Berg.
Plaintiff Theodore Vinther sworn and examined on his own behalf.
Exhibit 1. Notice of Pendency of Action. Id/Evid.
Exhibit 2. Notice of Proposed Action Independent Administration of Estates Act Consent filed on September 24, 202 in the San Francisco Action PES-12-295458. Id/Evid.After evidence.
Court signed Order Directing Title Company to Close Escrow on Purshase-Sale of Real Property.
Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiffs Theodore Vinther and Mary Vinther and against Defendant The Estate of Norman S. Wong through its Executor, Diane Berg.
The above-captioned action alleges a real property claim affecting certain real property that is situated in the City and County of San Francisco, State of California, and commonly refered to as 2200 Sacramento Street, Unit 1204, San Francisco, CA 95115 (the "Property").
The Plaintiff did not appear for Trial. Upon consideration of the evidence presented and for good cause show the Court hereby finds as follows:
1. The Plaintiffs are entitled to purchase the Property pursuant to the "Contract for the Sale and Purchase of Real Property" dated October 25, 2011. (the "Contract");
2. Plaintiffs have performed all terms and conditions required of them by the Contract, including the depositing of $$25,200.00 in Escrow Number 0221008453-MP at Old Republic Title Company;
3. That, upon depositing the cash balance of $814,800.00, escrow should be closed and the Property should be conveyed at that time to plaintiffs;
4. That Plaintiffs shall be awarded compensatory damages in the moung of $5,000.00 per month from December 15, 2011 through June 15, 2013.
5. That Plaintiffs shall be awarded costs of suit, which shall include attorney fees incurred herein pursuant to Paragraph 35 of the Contract:
Therefore, judgment is hereby entered in favor of plaintiffs and against defendant as follows:
1. That the Property be conveyed to plaintiffs in accordance with the terms of the Contract, and that the title company shall close escrow forthwith, and pursuant to a separately signed Order from this Court, when plaintiffs deposit the cash balance of $814,800.00 in escrow;
2. That plaintiffs shall recover $90,000.00 in compensatory damages from defendant, to accrue interest at the legal rate from the date of entry of judgment until paid;
3. That plaintiffs shall recover from defendant attorney fees and legal costs, in the amount of $22,750,00 to accrue interest at the legal rate from the date of entry of judgment until paid. Total Monetary Judgment: $112,750.00
Judgment signed in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2012 Set For Trial On Jun-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
